violations.text: At the time of inspection, the prep units in the kitchen area were observed to have tape being used to hold the inner top insulated portion of the lid in place. Per the PIC, the new lids are on order to replace the current lids. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/17/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar and kitchen dishwashers was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. At the time of inspection, bleach mixture discussed and properly set up for sanitizing at the 3 bay sink. Per the PIC, all dishes from bar and kitchen are to be washed at the kitchen 3 bay sink until dishwashers are properly setup/adjusted for chlorine sanitizer. PIC called for extra chemical from nearby location at the time of inspection. PIC called for service to the dish machines at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150 - 400 ppm. At the time of inspection, proper mixture of bleach sanitizer set up for use at the 3 bay sink. Per the PIC, bleach sanitizer solution will be used at the 3 bay sink until proper mixture of quaternary ammonium is setup/adjusted at he wall mounted mixer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

At the time of inspection, the prep units in the kitchen area were observed to have tape being used to hold the inner top insulated portion of the lid in place. Per the PIC, the new lids are on order to replace the current lids. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

At the time of inspection, pizza boxes were observed taped to the side of the glass door cooler next to the prep counter in the kitchen area as a way to prevent food debris from falling to the floor between equipment. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other FOOD soiling or that require frequent cleaning shall be constructed of a CORROSION-RESISTANT, nonabsorbent, and SMOOTH material. Per the PIC, the cardboard is to be replaced with a piece of sheet metal or plexiglass. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the cookline equipment were observed to have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2024

At the time of inspection, the pre-flush sprayer at the dish area was observed to be leaking at the faucet controls. Call was made for service at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

The floor in the lower level prep area is sealed with a coating that is observed to be peeling/chipping and no longer durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Per the PIC, a new floor covering/coating is on order and is to be installed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2024
